Notice Of Change Of Dept And Time For Hearing And Motion For Relief From Entry Of Default And To Set Aside Deault Judgemnt
Off Calendar Aug-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MONSTER MAGIC, LLC'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT is denied. The parties were served and failed to take appropriate steps to respond to the complaint. Defendants' declarations fail to establish a basis for excusable neglect. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Jun-03-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Douglas Holloway, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Bank of the West.
No appearances by defendants Monster Magic, LLC or Steven V. McVay.
Defaults entered against defendants on March 18, 2008.
Doe defendants dismissed on March 18, 2008.
Order waiving witness appearance at Default Prove-Up hearing was filed on April 25, 2008.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Bank of the West, and against the Defaulted Defendants: Monster Magic, LLC and Steven V. McVay as follows:

Damages/Principal - $125,084.87.
Prejudgment Interest - $5,860.17.
Attorney's Fees - $2,500.00.
Costs - $455.00, for a total of $133,900.04.

Additionally, Bank of the West is deemed the owner of the leased equipment described in Exhibit A of the judgment, and is entitled to immediate possession of said equipment.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
